stories.headline: No Deadline for Iran Proposal Amid Hormuz Standoff: US
stories.summary: The US and Iran are locked in a battle for control of the Strait of Hormuz after failing to meet for a fresh round of peace talks, with both sides blocking the waterway to gain leverage during an extended ceasefire. Bloomberg's Joumanna Bercetche reports from Dubai. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Diplomacy stalls as Iran fires on three ships in the Strait of Hormuz and US maintains blockade
stories.summary: Diplomatic talks between Iran and the United States appear to stall, with Tehran rejecting the premise of talks until Washington lifts its naval blockade of its ports. The international community has urged all parties to de-escalate and engage with diplomatic efforts to bring an end to the war.

stories.headline: Iran war live: Israel kills Lebanese journalist; Tehran-US talks stalled
stories.summary: Israeli attacks on Lebanon killed at least five people on Wednesday, including Lebanese journalist Amal Khalil.

stories.headline: Navy Secretary John Phelan Is Leaving the Pentagon and the Trump Administration
stories.summary: John Phelan is leaving the Pentagon after months of tension with Pete Hegseth and other Pentagon leaders. The tumult comes as the Navy has been engaged in war with Iran.

stories.headline: China’s Factory Hub Faces Gas Price Shock as War Tightens Supply
stories.summary: The Chinese industrial hub of Guangdong, an economic powerhouse comparable in size to South Korea, has seen some electricity prices almost double due in part to constraints on the supply of natural gas from the Middle East.

stories.headline: Tesla Boosts Spending Plan to $25 Billion in AI, Robotics Push
stories.summary: Tesla anticipates billions of dollars in additional spending this year to support Elon Musk’s ambitions to transform the electric-vehicle pioneer into an AI and robotics company. Ed Ludlow reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Indonesia Sells ¥172.1 Billion Samurai Bond on Strong Demand
stories.summary: Indonesia raised ¥172.1 billion ($1.1 billion) in its biggest Samurai bond sale in two years as strong investor demand for its credit outweighed concerns about budgetary pressures due to the Middle East war.

stories.headline: Kalshi Fines and Suspends 3 Political Candidates for Betting on Their Races
stories.summary: The prediction market said the candidates violated new rules. The platform and its competitors face growing scrutiny from lawmakers and regulators over how political betting is policed.

stories.headline: John Phelan forced out as Navy secretary after 13 months
stories.summary: The billionaire was a significant contributor to President Donald Trump’s campaign, but officials said he clashed repeatedly with Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth.

stories.headline: University of Queensland Press cancels children’s book over illustrator’s post on ‘Zionist framing’ of Bondi attack

stories.headline: SK Hynix Q1 profit jumps fivefold despite Middle East energy crisis
China carmaker Chery, delivery app Meituan dive into nuclear fusion research
Trampled Underfoot by a Stampede of Unicorns
stories.summary: The valuations behind a string of planned IPOs could drain life from stock indexes.

stories.headline: Indonesia Rupiah Falls Most in Seven Months on Rising Oil Prices
stories.summary: The Indonesian rupiah declined by the most in seven months as rising oil prices hurt sentiment for the nation’s assets.

stories.headline: AI Boom Sparks Rush Into Chinese Optical Stocks as Top Trade
stories.summary: Chinese optical stocks are attracting growing investor interest on expectations that rising demand for optical components critical to artificial intelligence will drive the next leg of sector outperformance.

stories.headline: JPMorgan to Add Philippine Bonds to Key EM Index in 2027
stories.summary: JPMorgan Chase & Co. said it will add local-currency bonds from the Philippines into its widely followed emerging-market index early next year, a move that may draw foreign investment and help spur the nation’s economy.

stories.headline: Australian Pensions Set to Boost Currency Hedging, Survey Shows
stories.summary: The vast majority of Australia’s pension funds intend to shield their portfolios from currency swings amid fears tensions in the Middle East will rapidly escalate, according to a survey by Commonwealth Bank of Australia.

stories.headline: Counterpoint: SK Hynix is Riding the Supercycle
stories.summary: Tom Kang, Research Director at Counterpoint, discusses the outlook for SK Hynix as the company reported a five-fold jump in quarterly profit and reiterated plans to ratchet up its capex this year. He speaks with Haidi Stroud-Watts and Paul Allen on Bloomberg: The Asia Trade. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Modi Seeks to Win Over Key India State After Voter Rolls Purged
stories.summary: Just two weeks before voters in India’s eastern state of West Bengal were due to go to the polls, about 9 million names were removed from the registered roll, causing an uproar among opposition groups.

stories.headline: Combative, Defensive and Occasionally Contrite, Kennedy Walks a Fine Line
stories.summary: In four days of congressional testimony, the health secretary sought to please the White House and his MAHA base at the same time.

stories.headline: Israeli forces fire tear gas at funeral of Palestinians killed by settlers
stories.summary: Israeli forces raided the funeral of two Palestinians killed by settlers at a school in the occupied West Bank.

stories.headline: Sexual violence in the West Bank emerges as tool to intimidate Palestinians
stories.summary: Systematic sexual violence and harassment by Israeli soldiers, settlers displacing Palestinians in occupied West Bank.

stories.headline: US and Iran in blockade standoff as Pakistan pushes for talks
stories.summary: The mood in the Strait of Hormuz remains combustible despite Trump's ceasefire extension.

stories.headline: Iran says it has seized two ships in Strait of Hormuz after vessels attacked
stories.summary: One of the cargo ships was 'heavily damaged' by Iranian forces, a British maritime agency reported on Wednesday.

stories.headline: Trump’s ‘dirty ceasefire’ tested as Iran hits shipping
stories.summary: Pause may put a lid on full-blown war without stopping hostilities or easing energy crisis

stories.headline: Trump eyes Iran deal with many of the trade-offs he blasted Obama for accepting
stories.summary: As U.S. envoys pursue peace talks, the president confronts persistent sticking points: sanctions relief, enrichment limits and the risk of empowering Tehran.

stories.headline: Turkiye making efforts to revive Russia-Ukraine talks, says Erdogan
stories.summary: Turkish president meets NATO chief as Kyiv asks Ankara to host a leaders' level meeting with Russia.

stories.headline: EU approves €90bn loan for Ukraine as pipeline is turned on ending deadlock
stories.summary: Ukraine says it has reopened the Druzhba pipeline, after months of stalemate over stalled oil supplies to neighbouring Hungary.

stories.headline: Trump buys time for Iran deal after frantic day of diplomacy
stories.summary: The US president's decision marked the second time in as many weeks he has backed off a threat to escalate the war, buying more time.

stories.headline: World's biggest condom maker set to raise prices due to Iran war
stories.summary: Malaysia-based Karex produces more than five billion condoms a year and supplies global brands like Durex and Trojan.

stories.headline: Tesla boosts spending plans to $25bn as Musk doubles down on AI bet
stories.summary: CEO warns investors to expect ‘very significant’ spending increase on self-driving taxis, trucks, robots and chip factories

stories.headline: How the War Powers Act Could Pressure Trump to End the Iran War
stories.summary: A decades-old law allows the president to wage war without congressional approval for 60 days, then limits his options for continuing. President Trump may seek to get around it.

stories.headline: El Salvador holds mass trial for 486 alleged members of notorious MS-13 gang
stories.summary: Human rights groups have warned that the collective prosecutions violate due process and block defendants from accessing legal counselA Salvadoran court on Tuesday began a collective trial of 486 alleged gang members, in one of the biggest mass trials under president Nayib Bukele’s crackdown on gang violence through controversial emergency powers.Prosecutors say the charges against alleged members of the Mara Salvatrucha gang, or MS-13, span more

stories.headline: Billionaire backer sues Trump family's crypto firm over alleged extortion
stories.summary: Billionaire investor Justin Sun is suing the family's World Liberty crypto venture after spending $45m on its tokens.

stories.headline: Nigeria's suspected coup plotters deny treason charges
stories.summary: Allegations of a coup plot first surfaced last year when an Independence Day parade was cancelled.

stories.headline: Taiwan president cancels trip after African countries revoke flight permits
stories.summary: Taiwan has accused Beijing of putting pressure on African countries to close their airspace to Lai's plane.

stories.headline: Virginia approves redistricting measure, boosting Democrats' hopes for midterms
stories.summary: The new maps approved by the state's voters could allow Democrats to flip as many as four House seats.

stories.headline: Crypto billionaire Justin Sun sues Trump family’s World Liberty Financial
stories.summary: Fraud claims come amid escalating feud between the Trump venture and one of its biggest supporters

stories.headline: How Kevin Warsh Is Distancing Himself From Trump
stories.summary: Lawmakers questioned the ability of Kevin Warsh, President Trump’s pick for Fed chair, to lead the central bank independently. Colby Smith explains how Mr. Warsh tried to distance himself from the president.

stories.headline: Clearing Strait of Hormuz of mines could take 6 months, Pentagon tells Congress
stories.summary: The Pentagon assessment, shared in a classified briefing for lawmakers, suggests gasoline and oil prices could remain elevated through the midterm elections.

stories.headline: Virginia passes redistricting measure that could help Democrats retake the House
stories.summary: The referendum creates as many as four Democratic-leaning districts in Virginia ahead of the midterm elections.

stories.headline: Heatwaves, floods and wildfires pose rising threat to democracy, report finds
stories.summary: Research shows natural hazards linked to climate crisis disrupted 23 elections in 18 countries in 2024Democracy is under mounting threat from the climate crisis, with new analysis documenting how elections are increasingly shaped not only by political forces but also by floods, wildfires and extreme weather.At least 94 elections and referendums across 52 countries have been disrupted by climate-related impacts over the last two decades, researche

stories.headline: Top ministers quit after Peru’s president postpones F-16 fighter jet deal
stories.summary: The Trump administration has warned against Peru pulling out of the deal, saying it reflects 'bad faith' negotiations.

stories.headline: Prediction market Kalshi docks three US candidates for betting on own races
stories.summary: Penalties come amid calls for greater oversight; company pledges to proactively police 'insider trading' on platforms.

stories.headline: Man City topple Arsenal at Premier League summit with nervy win at Burnley
stories.summary: Manchester City relegate Burnley with 1-0 win to also take top spot from Arsenal in the Premier League title race.

stories.headline: South Korean fighter jets collided due to pilots taking pictures, report finds
stories.summary: One of the pilots has been fined thousands of dollars by the military to cover repair costs.

stories.headline: Moldovan oligarch jailed in $1bn 'theft of the century' case
stories.summary: Vlad Plahotniuc, once Moldova's richest man, was found guilty of partaking in a fraud which involved around 12% of the country's GDP.

stories.headline: Britain’s desperate need for Keir Badenoch
stories.summary: The country requires centre-left foreign policy and centre-right reform at home

stories.headline: Stocks Fall With Bonds as Iran Concerns Boost Oil: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: Stocks and bonds dropped as stalled US-Iran talks and the continued closure of the Strait of Hormuz drove oil prices higher, souring risk sentiment after a record rally on Wall Street.

stories.headline: Suspect in slaying of Colombian presidential hopeful arrested in Argentina
stories.summary: Authorities say a Colombian suspected of helping to organise the killing of Miguel Uribe was captured in Buenos Aires.

stories.headline: Syria's president arrives in Qatar for talks after Saudi Arabia visit
stories.summary: Syria’s president Ahmed al-Sharaa visited Doha on Wednesday for talks with Qatar’s emir on economic cooperation, investment and post-al-Assad regional reintegration.

stories.headline: Deepfakes of frontline Ukrainian soldiers aim to undermine morale
stories.summary: Moscow is using AI-generated videos of Ukrainian soldiers to portray the country as weak and undermine trust in its central command.

stories.headline: 'Final rave': French ravers in Marseille fear harsh crackdown on free parties
stories.summary: In Marseille, France, revellers gathered to celebrate before the final adoption of the law tightening the penalties for ‘unauthorised parties’.

stories.headline: Two Palestinians killed during settler attack on West Bank village, officials say
stories.summary: A 14-year-old boy was among those shot dead, according to the mayor of al-Mughayyir.

stories.headline: Condom prices could rise 30% due to Iran war, says world’s top producer Karex
stories.summary: Karex produces more than 5 billion condoms annually and is a supplier to leading brands like Durex and Trojan, as well as the NHS The world’s top condom producer, Malaysia’s Karex Bhd, plans to raise prices by 20% to 30% and possibly further if supply chain disruptions drag on due to the Iran war, its chief executive has said.Karex is also seeing a surge in condom demand as rising freight costs and shipping delays have left many of its customers

stories.headline: Iran sees mass redundancies from war with US and Israel
stories.summary: The impact is being felt by manufacturers, retailers and the digital sector, amid warnings it could get worse if the war resumes.

stories.headline: From Epstein to sock puppets: Key takeaways from Kevin Warsh's Fed confirmation hearing
stories.summary: He denied making a deal with Donald Trump on interest rates and backed "policy regime change" at the central bank.

stories.headline: Robot chases wild boars off the streets of Warsaw
stories.summary: The Polish capital has seen the number of wild boar on its streets soar to more than 3,000 - a 20-fold increase since 2020.

stories.headline: Japan on high alert for 'huge' second quake after lifting tsunami warning
stories.summary: Japan's meteorological agency warns another, stronger earthquake may hit in the next week.

stories.headline: A Kashmir tourist hotspot became a bloodbath. A year on, the pain remains unbearable
stories.summary: A year after militants killed 26 people in Indian-administered Kashmir, families are trying to cope with their loss.

stories.headline: House Democrat resigns ahead of possible expulsion vote over alleged financial crimes
stories.summary: Rep. Sheila Cherfilus-McCormick was indicted last year on charges that she stole and laundered federal disaster money and used it to support a congressional campaign.

stories.headline: Taiwan president blames China for forced cancellation of Eswatini trip
stories.summary: Lai Ching-te abandons visit after Seychelles, Mauritius and Madagascar revoke overflight permission  Taiwan’s president, Lai Ching-te, has cancelled his trip to Eswatini, the democratic island’s only diplomatic ally in Africa, after his government said several countries had revoked overflight permits because of “intense pressure” from China.Lai was to leave on Wednesday for the 40th anniversary of King Mswati III’s accession. stories.headline: Unprecedented ruling finds Hungary's anti-LGBTQ laws in breach of EU values
stories.summary: The EU's top court finds that the reforms breached EU values on a number of levels and broke the founding values of the EU treaty.

stories.headline: Hotpot, bubble tea and sportswear: China's new exports take on the world
stories.summary: Made in China used to mean cheap, mass-produced goods but a new wave of Chinese brands is emerging.

stories.headline: Why police are seeking to arrest billionaire K-pop mogul behind BTS
stories.summary: Bang Si-hyuk, who created the supergroup, denies defrauding investors before his $7.3bn company went public.

stories.headline: Sam Neill says New Zealand goldmine supporters have threatened him with violence
stories.summary: Actor, who has publicly objected to plans to fast-track project near his farm, says he has received personal abuseThe actor Sam Neill says he has received threats of violence from supporters of a controversial goldmine that could be opened several kilometres away from his farm in New Zealand’s Central Otago district, after he publicly objected to the government’s plans to fast-track the mine.The Australian mining company Santana Minerals is pushi

stories.headline: Trump says US will not lift Hormuz blockade until deal made with Iran
stories.summary: The US president's comments come amid uncertainty over whether Iran will attend peace talks in Pakistan this week.

stories.headline: Rumen Radev's party wins Bulgarian election
stories.summary: With 87% of the vote now counted, Progressive Bulgaria has secured a majority in parliament.

stories.headline: India has splurged billions on metro trains. But where are the commuters?
stories.summary: Without better last-mile connectivity and affordable fares, metro use is unlikely to improve quickly, say experts.

stories.headline: Chernobyl's last wedding: The couple who married as a nuclear disaster unfolded
stories.summary: Serhiy and Iryna married 40 years ago, unaware a nuclear reactor had exploded less than 3 miles away.

stories.headline: A moment of risk and opportunity: How two ceasefires could boost US-Iran talks
stories.summary: Some kind of diplomatic progress is now under way, but is it enough to prevent a return to war?
